title: Runebound Retrospective - Episode 2 - The Island of Dread
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zksWnpcM05I

Daniel returns to explore the Island of Dread, Runebound's first major expansion—and a masterclass in how art and theme can transform a familiar system into something entirely new. This nautical-themed expansion captures something primal: the allure of discovering uncharted islands, sailing mysterious seas, and pursuing an awakened evil from the Lost Age. The new map overlay, filled with sea lanes and ports, invites you to hire sea captains and brave the legendary Sidor Sea.

What makes Island of Dread special is how it layers theme atop mechanics without bloating the ruleset. New legendary items, quest cards that reveal themselves through exploration, and events that reshape the map geometry create narrative momentum. Daniel's playthrough with Lysa of the East showcases the unexpected drama—getting shipwrecked, hunting down stolen weapons, and using sea captains strategically. The expansion proves that sometimes the smallest changes in setting and flavor can breathe new life into a game you thought you already knew.

----

Have you explored any of the big-box Runebound expansions, and did one particular theme grab you the way it did for Daniel?
